DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LEERAN SCHWARTZ'S MOTION TO AUGMENT EXPERT WITNESS DISCLOSURES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AUGMENT HIS EXPERT WITNESS DISCLOSURES IS DENIED. THE FACTS SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFF'S THE MOVING PAPERS ARE INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE STATUTORILY REQUIRED FINDINGS TO ALLOW DESIGNATION OF THE TWO ADDITIONAL EXPERTS. THE PLAINTIFF MAY SERVE A SUPPLEMENTAL EXPERT DESIGNATION AND DECLARATION CHANGING DR. NOAH WEISS FROM NON-RETAINED TO RETAINED. THE DEFENDANT MAY THEN AMEND ITS EXPERT LIST TO ADD AND EXPERT FROM THE SAME MEDICAL SPECIALTY. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND INCLUDE CHANGES RE: NAMED EXPERTS APPROVED AS TO FORM DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Augment Expert Witness Disclosures
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom videoconference):
Ike Kaludi, Esq. 415-967-4900 of California Trial Law Group for Plaintiff.
Cecilia Leineweber, Esq. 818-712-9800 cleineweber@bremerwhyte.com of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara for Defendant Thajali.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides argue. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEERAN SCHWARTZ's Motion To Augment Expert Witness Disclosures.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to augment his expert witness disclosures is denied. The facts set forth in plaintiff's the moving papers are insufficient to support the statutorily required findings to allow designation of the two additional experts. The plaintiff may serve a Supplemental Expert Designation and Declaration changing Dr. Noah Weiss from non-retained to retained. The defendant may then amend its expert list to add and expert from the same medical specialty. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is denied.

Defendant to prepare a proposed order and include changes re: named experts approved as to form directly to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
